title: South Korea as a Current Institutional Proof Point
subtitle: National dictionary publication, current institutional use, and why Korea matters
summary: South Korea shows SignWriting functioning inside current public dictionary infrastructure connected to a national-language institution.
synopsis: South Korea is a current institutional proof point for SignWriting. Korea is not the broad empirical center that Brazil is, but it shows that SignWriting can function inside current public dictionary infrastructure connected to a national-language institution.
cover_image_alt: A dictionary and institutional archive scene with SignWriting-like symbols, public-record cards, and a subtle Korea-focused evidence network.

slides.title: Why Korea Matters
slides.summary: South Korea is a current institutional proof point, not a broad empirical center like Brazil.

image_prompts.concrete: Clean institutional evidence slide highlighting South Korea, a national dictionary context, and current public SignWriting use
image_prompts.abstract: A current institutional marker standing beside a larger evidence map

slides.speech: South Korea matters because it gives the series a current institutional proof point. The claim is intentionally narrow. Korea is not presented as the broad empirical center that Brazil is; it shows that SignWriting can function inside current public dictionary infrastructure connected to a national-language institution.
slides.notes: Do not inflate the claim. The narrowness is what makes it credible.

slides.title: Institutional Marker
slides.summary: The February 6, 2025 event gives the evidence series a dated public marker for current institutional activity.

image_prompts.concrete: Timeline slide with February 6 2025 marked as a public institutional evidence point for SignWriting in South Korea
image_prompts.abstract: A dated marker placed on a calm institutional timeline

slides.speech: The February 6, 2025 marker matters because evidence work benefits from dates and public institutional context. It gives the platform a current point of reference without requiring the reader to infer a larger adoption story than the record supports.
slides.notes: Use the date to make the evidence concrete.

slides.title: Dictionary Evidence
slides.summary: The dictionary context shows SignWriting operating as public language infrastructure rather than only as private notation.

image_prompts.concrete: Academic slide showing dictionary entries, signed-language resources, and SignWriting symbols as part of public language infrastructure
image_prompts.abstract: Dictionary pages connected to a public language infrastructure node

bullets: Dictionary infrastructure is organized language work
Institutional context strengthens the proof point
The dictionary claim is not an outcome claim
slides.speech: The dictionary context matters because dictionaries are public language infrastructure. When SignWriting appears in that context, it becomes more than an isolated example. It becomes evidence of organized use in a public resource environment.
slides.notes: Keep dictionary evidence separate from literacy outcome claims.

slides.title: Limits Of The Claim
slides.summary: Korea supports a current institutional proof-point claim, not a general claim about national adoption or learning outcomes.

image_prompts.concrete: Evidence-boundary slide showing a clear box labeled current institutional proof point, with broader claims outside the box
image_prompts.abstract: A clear boundary around one strong but narrow evidence claim

bullets: Not a replacement for Brazil as empirical center
slides.speech: The Korea claim is strongest when it stays bounded. It does not prove national adoption, literacy outcomes, or broad community preference. It does show current public institutional use inside national dictionary infrastructure.
slides.notes: End with the bounded claim. Strong, narrow, useful.
